request: Bugfix in .claude/skills/audit-baseline/audit.sh: empty canonical memory files (frontmatter preamble only, zero record entries) should NOT cause the audit to fail. A freshly-initialized project legitimately has empty memory files. The audit must assert each canonical memory file exists and has a valid frontmatter preamble; record count is not an audit signal.

completed_notes.chore: Single-file audit.sh bugfix (entry-count assertion relaxed for canonical memory files; preamble-only PASSes). +5 backlog.md entries from this session's user-stated future-work intent.
completed_notes.integrate: chore-track: ran inlined verify (bash audit.sh, exit 0). simplify in exceptions (chore conditional triggers did not fire: single-file ~10-LOC edit, no refactor moves). Cross-engine smoke skipped: diff doesn't touch UI globs.
completed_notes.security: 1 LOW finding (A08 / CWE-345) — relaxed frontmatter check allows missing closer; non-blocking. Report at docs/security/audit-empty-memory-files-2026-05-17.md.
completed_notes.document: Excepted: no user-facing prose changed, no counts/inventories changed, no new conventions, no src/ templates touched, no seed.md §14/§16 entry resolved.
